children.content:
The Repeater is a context provider — contexts can be attached directly to it. This creates an easier mental model: the repeater is the list. The user binds the Items property to consume content.

When a list context is attached and Items is unbound, it is automatically bound to the context's array field — no extra step needed.

The user can also bind Items to a context that lives on a parent (section/page) — the repeater doesn't have to own a context.
children.name: overviewText

children.content: Contexts attach directly to the repeater. Reduces reparenting issues on D&D.
children.name: r1det

children.content: Shows repeater's own context with "This repeater" scope badge. Also shows parent contexts.
children.name: r2det

children.content: Items property auto-bound to context's array field when list context attached.
children.name: r3det

children.content: Same context type at parent and child levels. Closest instance wins (proximity resolution).
children.name: r4det

children.content: Coupled context → duplicated. Parent context → not duplicated. Paste out of context → disconnected.
children.name: r5det

children.content: Configured via repeater settings → "Edit context instance" floating panel. Applies to main records array.
children.name: r6det

children.content: From add panel (e.g., Products Gallery). Context exists on page / No context on page.
children.name: c1desc

children.content: Sort by title A/Z. Filter out if description is empty.
children.name: c2desc

children.content: Promote context to section so external controls can bind to it.
children.name: c3desc

children.content: Switch to a different context already on the page to feed the repeater.
children.name: c4desc

children.content: Select context: from existing, or create a new one.
children.name: c5desc

children.content: Show 2 repeaters with different configuration of the same context.
children.name: c6desc

children.content: User drags a pre-bound repeater (e.g., Products Gallery) from the Add Panel onto a section. Behavior depends on whether a matching context already exists on the page.
children.name: f1noteText

children.content: Inherit from parent. No context added to the repeater. Items auto-bind to the parent's context array.
children.name: sr1d

children.content: Context added on the repeater. Repeater owns it. Items auto-bind to the new context's array field.
children.name: sr2d

children.content: Sort and filter are configured on the context instance, not on the repeater directly. Accessed via repeater settings → "Edit context instance" (floating panel).
children.name: f2noteText

children.content: Select field "title" → direction "Ascending". Applies to the main records array. Repeater items reorder immediately.
children.name: ar1d

children.content: Add filter rule: field "description" → condition "is not empty". Items that don't match are hidden from repeater.
children.name: ar2d

children.content: If the repeater uses a parent context, sort/filter is also accessible via section settings → context configuration. Applies only to the main records array — nested arrays (e.g., tags) are not affected.
children.name: f2note2Text

children.content: User-on-User (UoU) interactions: sort, filter, pagination, total items. These require the context to be promoted to section level so external controls can bind to context-level data.
children.name: f3noteText

children.content: Message suggests to promote when context is coupled to repeater.
children.name: f3r1d

children.content: Shows the repeater's context with a "Promote to section" action.
children.name: f3r2d

children.content: Same requirement applies to any context-level data outside repeater items — e.g., itemsCount, total items, current filter state.
children.name: f3warnText

children.content: User switches the repeater to consume a different context that already exists on the page, instead of its own or current context.
children.name: f4noteText

children.content: The context picker shows: repeater's own context (with "This repeater" badge), parent contexts (section/page), and page-level contexts. The user picks any available context and selects which array field to bind Items to.
children.name: f4hintText

children.content: User drags an unconnected repeater (e.g., from Quick Add). It has no context — user can design freely and connect later.
children.name: f5noteText

children.content: "Use from page/section." Repeater references parent's context — no new context created.
children.name: f5r1d

children.content: New context created on the repeater. Exists only for it.
children.name: f5r2d

children.content: If user tries to bind inner elements before the repeater's Items has data → suggest auto-binding the repeater to the selected array alongside the element binding.
children.name: f5hintText

children.content: Two repeaters on the same section can share the same context type but have different configurations (different sort, filter, page size). Each repeater owns its own context instance.
children.name: f6noteText

children.content: Each repeater owns its own context instance. Changes to one repeater's sort/filter/page size do not affect the other. This is possible because context lives on the repeater (Decision #1).
children.name: f6archText

children.content: Repeater is a context provider — contexts attach directly to it.
children.name: fr1d

children.content: Items auto-bound to array field when list context attached.
children.name: fr2d

children.content: Shows repeater's own context (default), parent contexts, scope badge.
children.name: fr3d

children.content: Coupled context moves with repeater. Inherited: choose copy/promote/disconnect.
children.name: fr4d

children.content: If user binds inner element before Items has data, suggest auto-binding.
children.name: fr5d

children.content: What to show when repeater is unbound on live site — collapse, placeholder, or hide.
children.name: fr6d

children.content: Paste out of context — repeater pasted where no matching context exists → elements are disconnected. TBD: editor experience.
children.name: ec1text

children.content: Drag to different section (inherited context) — context stays in original section. User must choose: copy to new section / promote to page / disconnect.
children.name: ec2text

children.content: Nested arrays — sub-arrays do not have context-level sort/filter/page size. User needs to bind to a function that returns a filtered/sorted array.
children.name: ec3text

children.content: Blank repeater + inner element bind — if Items has no data and user tries to bind an inner element, suggest auto-binding the repeater first.
children.name: ec4text

children.content: Context-level vs item-level fields — binding UI should clearly indicate when a field is "Same value across all rows" (context-level).
children.name: ec5text

children.content: System contexts on repeater — not allowed. System contexts (User, Site, Router) appear only at page level.
children.name: ec6text

children.content:
The editor canvas in its initial state.
Hero section and weather data are visible.

User clicks + to open the Add Elements panel.
Repeater presets appear: Blank, Articles, Team.

User selects "Articles" (pre-bound repeater).
It's auto-placed and connected to data.

The repeater's context is auto-bound.
Pink pill: "Articles repeater added & connected".

Context pill shows "Repeater | S Articles · articles".
Items are auto-bound to the Articles context.

Full canvas: hero, weather, and Articles repeater
all visible. Data is live and connected.
